commit.message:
fix: address second-pass P1/P2 review comments

projectionEngine.ts:
- Remove unused PENSION_RULES and INCOME_TAX imports
- Add CURRENT_TAX_YEAR_START to imports
- Replace new Date().getFullYear() + y with CURRENT_TAX_YEAR_START + y
  in the simulation loop so the tax year is deterministic and correctly
  aligned to the UK April boundary regardless of the runtime clock

taxCalculations.ts:
- Import CURRENT_TAX_YEAR_START from financialConstants
- Replace calendarYear ?? new Date().getFullYear() with
  calendarYear ?? CURRENT_TAX_YEAR_START in all three functions
  (calcIncomeTax, isHigherRateTaxpayer, calcCGT)

tests/unit/taxCalculations.test.ts:
- Add const Y = 2025 to calcIncomeTax, isHigherRateTaxpayer, and calcCGT
  describe blocks and pass Y as explicit calendarYear to every function
  call in those suites — ensures tests are pinned to a known tax year
  and will not become flaky as the real calendar year advances

docs/hmrc-tax-mcp-implementation.md:
- Replace stale TaxYearSnapshot flat-record shape with the actual
  IncomeTaxBands / CgtSnapshot / PensionSnapshot / ResolvedSnapshot
  interface definitions that match the generated code
- Replace stale getSnapshotForYear signature (returned TaxYearSnapshot)
  with the real signature including memoization cache, deduped warn
  helper, and cgtFallback / pensionFallback flags
- Update financialConstants example to use CURRENT_TAX_YEAR_START
  instead of new Date().getFullYear()

  and correct snapshot field paths (snapshot.pension.lsa etc.)

commit.message:
ci: add validate-tax-rules job for HMRC snapshot drift detection

Add a new CI job that:
  1. Regenerates src/config/taxRuleSnapshot.ts via npm run gen:tax-snapshot
  2. Asserts the regenerated file matches the committed snapshot (git diff --exit-code)
     — fails the build if the committed snapshot is stale
  3. Runs npx vitest run tests/unit/taxCalculations.test.ts to confirm all
     tax calculation tests pass against the current snapshot

Also add validate-tax-rules to merge-gate prerequisites so PRs cannot merge
with a stale or inconsistent snapshot.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(tax): add HMRC worked examples and snapshot fallback tests

Extend taxCalculations.test.ts with:

1. HMRC income tax worked examples (calendarYear: 2025, tax year 2025-26):
   - £10,000 → £0 (below PA)
   - £30,000 → £3,486 (basic rate: verified against hmrc-local)
   - £75,000 → £17,432 (basic + higher rate: verified against hmrc-local)
   - £110,000 → £33,432 (PA tapered to £7,570: verified against hmrc-local)
   - £130,000 → £44,703 (PA fully tapered; additional rate: verified against hmrc-local)

2. CGT worked examples (calendarYear: 2025, tax year 2025-26):
   - £2,000 gain → £0 (below exempt)
   - £10,000 gain, basic rate → £1,260 (18% on £7,000)
   - £10,000 gain, higher rate → £1,680 (24% on £7,000)

3. Snapshot fallback tests:
   - getSnapshotForYear(2025): exact 2025-26 entry; cgtFallback=false
   - getSnapshotForYear(2030): exact 2030-31 income tax entry; cgt falls back
     to 2026-27 with cgtFallback=true (documented expected behaviour)

Also update the existing test 'income at additional-rate threshold → PA fully
tapered to £0': fix the expected formula to match the HMRC rule (basic band
width = 37,700, not 50,270 when PA is 0). The corrected formula gives £42,516
for income £125,140 — consistent with hmrc-local income_tax_due output.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  - Replace PENSION_RULES.UFPLS_TAX_FREE_FRACTION with yearSnapshot.pension.ufplsTaxFreeFraction
    at all six usage sites.

values for that year match the previous hardcoded constants.

commit.message:
feat(tax): add calendarYear param to tax functions; use HMRC snapshot formula

Modify calcIncomeTax, isHigherRateTaxpayer, and calcCGT to accept an optional
calendarYear parameter (defaults to current year) and look up the correct HMRC
rule snapshot for that year via getSnapshotForYear(calendarYear).

Income tax formula now matches the HMRC income_tax_due rule (v1.0.0):
  - Basic rate band width is always (basicRateLimit − personalAllowance) = £37,700.
  - When the personal allowance is tapered, additional income flows into the
    higher rate band, creating the 60% effective marginal rate in the
    £100,000–£125,140 taper zone (as per HMRC published guidance).

All three functions remain backward-compatible: existing call sites that
omit calendarYear continue to work using the current calendar year.
drawFromGIA is unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(tax): wire financialConstants to HMRC snapshot for current year

Replace hardcoded CGT and pension constants with values sourced from the
HMRC tax rule snapshot for the current calendar year:
  - CGT.ANNUAL_EXEMPT  → snapshot.cgt.exemptAmount
  - CGT.BASIC_RATE     → snapshot.cgt.basicRate
  - CGT.HIGHER_RATE    → snapshot.cgt.higherRate
  - PENSION_RULES.PCLS_LUMP_SUM_ALLOWANCE   → snapshot.pension.lsa
  - PENSION_RULES.UFPLS_TAX_FREE_FRACTION   → snapshot.pension.ufplsTaxFreeFraction

Constant names are unchanged so all existing imports continue to work.
Multi-year projections that need per-year values should call
getSnapshotForYear(calendarYear) directly (as projectionEngine.ts now does).

commit.message:
feat(tax): add HMRC tax rule snapshot and generator (Phase 1)

Add per-group typed snapshot file (src/config/taxRuleSnapshot.ts) covering:
  - income_tax_bands: 2025-26 through 2030-31
  - cgt_due: 2025-26 and 2026-27 only
  - pension_lsa / UFPLS: 2025-26 and 2026-27 only
  - state_pension_annual: 2025-26 and 2026-27

The snapshot generator (scripts/gen-tax-snapshot.ts) hard-codes the known
rule output values from hmrc-tax-mcp. Running npm run gen:tax-snapshot inside
the Copilot CLI environment re-emits the snapshot. Rule values verified via
hmrc-local-execute_rule as at 2026-04-03.

getSnapshotForYear(calendarYear) provides per-year lookup with per-group
fallback to the latest confirmed entry. console.warn is emitted when a
fallback is used (suppressed in NODE_ENV=test). cgtFallback and
pensionFallback flags allow callers to act on the fallback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

orchestrator: 9 quality improvements from agent architecture analysis

1. Model-level fallback: retry entire ReAct loop with fallback model
   when primary fails after per-call retries are exhausted
2. Structured error classification: replace string-matching with
   isinstance checks against litellm exception classes
3. DAG parallel subtask isolation: document and enforce context
   isolation for concurrent sub-tasks
4. Nudge message cleanup: pop complete_task nudge messages after use
   to avoid polluting subsequent turn context
5. Enhanced loop detection: check tool name + args fingerprint +
   result hash (3 layers) instead of just tool name
6. Smart summarization truncation: token-budget-aware per-message
   truncation preserving head+tail instead of 500-char hard cut
7. DAG synthesis with user context: inject user profile and language
   preference into result synthesis LLM call
8. Unified tool pipeline: ToolPipeline with before/after hooks for
   credential checks and audit logging
9. Plan state persistence: PlanStore backed by PostgreSQL so pending
   plans survive process restarts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use short lease with background renewal thread

Replace invalid 300s / infinite lease with 30s finite lease + daemon
thread that renews every 10s. If the function crashes, the lock
auto-expires in 30s instead of being stuck forever.

commit.message:
feat(dirpicker): replace filepicker with list component (dirs-only + search) (#6)

* fix(dirpicker): replace DidSelectFile with fp.Path diff for reliable selection

DidSelectFile checks m.files[m.selected] after Update, but m.files is still
the old parent listing because readDir is async. When m.selected resets to 0
and the first entry of the parent is a file, FileAllowed=false makes it return
false, so selection is never emitted and the user gets stuck.

Fix: capture fp.Path before calling fp.Update, then compare afterwards. A
change in fp.Path means a directory was selected — no dependency on the stale
files slice.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(dirpicker): set explicit height so file list is visible

AutoHeight=true relies on WindowSizeMsg being delivered to the filepicker,
but that message was intentionally excluded from the dirPicker routing block
(to prevent it from swallowing the resize before app.go processes it).
Result: filepicker never learned the terminal height and defaulted to 0.

Fix: disable AutoHeight, add SetHeight(termHeight) that computes
listHeight = termHeight - overlayOverhead (10 lines for border, padding,
header, path, separator, footer). Call it from the WindowSizeMsg handler
so the picker always fills the available overlay space.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: add go-humanize transitive dep for bubbles/filepicker

Required by github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/filepicker for file size
display. Added with go get github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/filepicker@v1.0.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(dirpicker): replace filepicker with list component, dirs-only + search

bubbles/filepicker has no way to hide non-selectable entries; files always
render as disabled/grayed-out rows even with FileAllowed=false.

Replace with a custom wrapper around bubbles/list:
- os.ReadDir filtered to IsDir() only — files never appear
- list.SetFilteringEnabled(true) gives built-in fuzzy search-as-you-type
- enter descends into highlighted directory and selects it
- h/←/backspace navigates up one level
- . confirms current directory without descending
- esc clears active filter, or cancels picker when not filtering
- height is sized from SetHeight(termHeight) to fill the overlay

Remove bubbles/filepicker import and go-humanize transitive dep (no longer
needed). No changes to app.go — DirPickedMsg/DirPickerCancelMsg unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(dirpicker): rebuild list from scratch on each directory navigation

The previous approach used dp.list.SetItems() (pointer receiver mutation) to
update the list items. However, list.Model.Update() has a value receiver and
returns a new Model — so dp.list was constantly being replaced by
dp.list.Update(msg), which discarded any SetItems mutations made between
Update calls. The result: the list always showed the initial directory.

Fix: store the delegate and height on DirPicker. On each loadDir(), recreate
the list.Model from scratch via buildList(). This guarantees the displayed
items always match the current directory. No stale state possible.

Also: Show() no longer returns a tea.Cmd (no async init needed); update both
call sites in app.go accordingly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address Copilot review comments on dirpicker PR

- Don't drop background messages while dir picker is active; only
  short-circuit when the picker actually consumes the message
- Add m.dirPicker.Active to handleMouse modal guard so mouse events
  are blocked while the picker is open
- Explicitly deactivate dirPicker in DirPickedMsg and DirPickerCancelMsg
  handlers as a defensive measure
- Handle non-IsNotExist os.Stat errors in DirPickedMsg by surfacing an
  ErrorMsg instead of silently proceeding
- Handle os.ReadDir errors in DirPicker.loadDir; keep previous dir on
  failure and show error message in View
- Fix CHANGELOG 'Changed' entry: was incorrectly saying migration was
  to bubbles/filepicker; now correctly says custom DirPicker on bubbles/list
- Fix CHANGELOG 'Added' entry: clarify that filter mode requires pressing
  '/' first, not 'type any characters'
- Add unit tests: new-project flow, DirPickedMsg (existing dir),
  DirPickedMsg (non-existent dir), DirPickerCancelMsg, background
  messages not dropped while picker is active
